title: IMD issues Red Alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall across several states - News On AIR
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall over Gujarat, Konkan, Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Odisha and West Madhya Pradesh till tomorrow. It has also predicted thunderstorms accompanied by lightning over Chhattisgarh, East Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha.
Akashvani correspondent reports that according to IMD, heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected over Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Andhra Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal, Karnataka, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Karaikal, Telangana and the North Eastern parts of the country till tomorrow. Similar conditions will also prevail over Chhattisgarh, Coastal Karnataka, East Madhya Pradesh, East Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh, Kerala, Mahe, Uttarakhand and Vidarbha for the day.
The weather agency noted that thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds is likely to prevail over Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, Chandigarh, Rajasthan, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Gujarat and Jharkhand till tomorrow. Meanwhile, the Met department has said that Arunachal Pradesh, Assam and Meghalaya will experience hot and humid conditions till Monday.
